activities.markdown:
# Ghost Notes: The Invisible Groove

Ghost notes are extremely quiet snare hits — so soft they're barely audible, felt more as a presence than heard as distinct notes. They're the secret ingredient in deep funk, R&B, and hip-hop drumming.

**What ghost notes do:**
- Fill the space between accent notes with constant 16th-note subdivision
- Create density and 'pocket' feel without adding volume
- Give the groove an 'organic' quality — it sounds like a human drummer rather than a drum machine
- Allow the accent notes to project more clearly by contrast

**Technique:**
- Stick height: 1/2 to 1 inch above the head
- Velocity: barely make contact — the stick almost falls onto the head
- No wrist snap — the stick 'droops' onto the head under its own weight
- The ghost note must be even in volume — inconsistent ghosts are worse than no ghosts

**The ghost note scale:** Think of your snare dynamics as a 10-point scale. Ghosts are 1-2. Normal backbeats are 7. Rim shots are 10. Funk drumming explores the full range.

activities.title: Ghost Note Funk Pattern
activities.description: Ghost notes (o) fill every 16th note position around the main accents (X). The accent on beat 2 and beat 4 contrast sharply with the surrounding ghosts. Hi-hat drives steady 16th notes. Kick on beat 1 and the 'and' of 2.

activities.question: What is the primary musical function of ghost notes in funk drumming?
choices: To add volume and fill the room with sound
To create continuous 16th-note subdivision density and pocket without adding dynamic weight
activities.explanation: Ghost notes fill the rhythmic space between accent notes at an extremely soft dynamic, creating the sense of constant 16th-note activity that gives funk its distinctive pocket feel. They add density and organic quality without competing with the main accents.
